## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hey everyone, welcome back to another Quick Start tutorial. So in today's session we're going to be talking about social media list building. Now never before in the history of the web has it been easier to build a targeted following using such social media channels as Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Google+, Pinterest, YouTube. The list goes on. And so in our session today our focus is going to be on how do you build up a targeted following within these social media channels. And then from there how do you generate email leads, how do you build your list using social media. And so we're going to start by talking about a social media list building sequence. And we're going to cover three different categories of lists that are important to build into your business. And second of all we're going to uncover four steps to building your list using social media. And then I'm going to kind of introduce a case study that will reinforce what we're talking about today. So let's get started. So these are three core lists that you certainly want to be building into your business. The first one on the left is your social media lists. This could be your blog subscribers, whether that be RSS or email subscribers, your Facebook friends or your Facebook page likes, your Twitter followers, your Google+, connections, your YouTube subscribers, your LinkedIn connections, and those that are subscribing to your Pinterest content. Those are your social media lists and it's relatively easy to build up a pretty targeted following within social media pretty quickly. And I'm going to show you a demo on how to do that here in a moment. But our primary goal with building up our social media following is yes, is to build community, is to share great content, is to add value. But we ultimately want to see those social media connections being added to our prospect email lists. That could be done by sharing squeeze pages through our social media channel, putting out newsletters, product launches, maybe starting the sequence using our social media channels, hosting events, running surveys, all with the purpose of building up our email list. Because this is your low hanging fruit, you've already connected in social media with a real targeted following. Now you want to bring them onto your list because that is a tangible asset in your business, one that you own. And then from there, you want to be able to convert them from your prospect list over to your buyer list. And as Jeff mentioned earlier in the program, someone on your buyer's list is worth 10 times plus more than your prospect list and certainly way more than your social media list. So that's the sequence that you want to be thinking about. Yes, it's great to have a whole ton of social media connections, but you want your purpose, yes, it's great to have a whole ton of social media connections, but ultimately your goal is to bring them over and add them to your email list and ultimately your buyer's list. So as I mentioned, not all lists are certainly created equal.

## [03:01 - 06:08]

The buyer's list is worth 10 times more than your prospect list and way more than your social media lists. All right, so let's talk about four steps to building your social media list. First of all, you want to identify the right social networks. There's a lot of great ones out there right now and it's easy to get overwhelmed with diving into too many. So you want to pick the ones where you think your audience will be hanging out and that you can pump out some real relevant content. Step two is to actually build a targeted following. And then here in a moment, I'm going to show you how easy it is now to quickly build a following within these different social media channels. Then step three is to publish relevant content and engage the community. So you want to be pumping out all forms of content, video and relevant tips and blog posts and all sorts of different types of content that is real relevant to your audience and then you're interacting with them on that content. You're responding to their questions and their comments and you're fully engaged with your community. It's incredibly important that you're consistently adding value to your social media community. And then step four is to share offers and promotions. Certainly, that is done as you build credibility, as you add great value to your community. You can then strategically put in some free offers, launch products through social media, share some special discounts and promotions, all that good stuff. It certainly has to be a part of your overall game plan. So those are four steps that we're going to be talking about today. So first of all, what social network should I focus on? These are six of the biggest right now and certainly you want to be able to focus in on the ones that fit your audience, where your audience is most hanging out. So if your audience is kind of an artsy audience, maybe you're a professional chef or you're in the fitness space or different things like that, Pinterest is huge right now for that particular market. If you have a B2B product, LinkedIn is definitely a place you want to be. Facebook kind of transcends all sorts of markets. So certainly you want to be on Facebook, Twitter the same. YouTube obviously online video is one of the most popular mediums right now. So certainly one that you want to focus on. And Google Plus is rising quickly. But I would encourage you to remember that less is always more. You don't want to jump into all six of these right away. You might focus on building up your Facebook page or building up your following in Twitter and doing those well and then maybe adding one after that, adding YouTube or adding Pinterest. But to try to focus on all three or four at the same time right out of the starter gate, that's where you can kind of get overwhelmed and then your social media efforts kind of fall off the rails. So be thinking about that, that less is more. You can really build up a strong presence just using Facebook or just using YouTube.

Let's say that you are putting out a new and viral-friendly cleaning product that you want to, you want to build your list, you want to get a list of followers, you want to get certainly a list of buyers, but first of all you are trying to build up your social media connections. And what you can do in order to get connected with people that are in the eco-friendly space or their eco-friendly enthusiasts is to go to the search feature within Facebook and you can filter it by searching by pages or people or public posts which is seeing that the people that are actually talking about green topics, those are the people that you certainly want to become friends with. And so I have it on pages right now and so this is a listing of a variety of eco-friendly pages. You could be searching the term green and other related terms as well. ## [15:13 - 17:02]

offer or subscribe to receive more blog content from you. And this is an example of how it might look. That will basically raise your credibility levels with your community. Practice the 80-20 principle when it comes to promotion. Certainly you don't want to go in just promote, here's this free ebook, buy this $7 product. That's all you're sharing then people are going to get fatigued with your and frustrated with your content and stop following you. You want to be sharing 80% of the time real great valuable content and interacting with people and then strategically that 20% putting in free offers, low-cost products, product launches, things like that.
